T, Wy Wt -- 3 of 7 -- THEGAZETTEOF INDIA : EXTRAORDINAR Y [Parr II—Sec. 3()] MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT AND FORESTS NOTIFICATION . New Delhi, the 17th January, 2007 . G.S.R. 29(E). —In exercise of the powers conferred by clause (e) of sub- section 2 of "settion 63 of the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974, (8 of 1974) and in supesession of the Chairman, Central Poliution Control Board Recruitment Rules,2006,except as respects things done ar omitted to be done before such supersession, the Central Government hereby makes the following rules for regulating the terms and conditions of service of the Chairman in the Central Pollution Control Board namely:- 1. Short title and commencement~(1) These rules may be called the Chairman Central Pollution Control Board Recruitment Rules, 2006. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Number of the said posts, their classification and the scale of pay attached thereto shall be as specified in columns 2 lo + of the Schedule annexed to these rules. . 3. Method of recruitment, age limit, qualification, pay, allowances, leave and other conditions of service etc of Chairman Central Pollution Control Board — (1) The scale of pay of Chairman shall be as specified in column ‘4 of Schedule. {2) The rules and orders for the time being in force applicable to an officer holding posts of the corresponding scale of pay under the Central Government, shall regulate the conditions of service in respect of allowance, leave, joining time, joining time pay, provident fund, gratuity, age of superannuation, retirement benefits, medical facilities and other conditions of service of the Chairman of the Board. . 4. Method of recruitment, age limits, qualification etc:-The method of recruitment, age limit, qualification and other matters relating thereto shall be as specified in columns 5 to 14 of the said Schedule 5. Disqualification:- No Person, (a) who has entered into or contracted a marriage with a person having a spouse living, or {b} who having a spouse living has entered into or contracted a mamiage with any person, . shall be eligible for appointment to the said post. Provided that the Central Government may, if satisfied that such marriage permissible under personal law applicable to such person or the other party to the marriage and that there are other grounds for so doing, exempt any person from the operation of this rule. o 6. Power to relax - Where the Central Government is of the opinion that it is necessary or expedient to do so, it may by order for reasons to be recorded in writing, refax any of the provisions of these rules with respect to any class of category of persons. 7 Saving:- Nothing in these rules shall affect reservation, refaxation of age limit and other concessions required to be provided for Schedule Caste/Schedule Tribe/Other Backward Classes/Ex-Servicemen and other speciai categories of persons in accordance with the orders issues by the Central Govemment from time to time in this regard. -- 4 of 7 -- Tem-Contract) In- case of recrutment by promotion/deputation/absorptio -| n, grades from which promotion/ deputation/absorption fo be made If @ Departmental Promotion Committee exists what is its composition Circumstances in which Union Public Service .Commission is to be consuited m making recrutment 12 13 14 Officers under the Central/State Govemments/Public Sector Undertakings/Universities/ Recognized Research Institutions or Autonomous or Semi Govemment.
